governanceNote: NGW operationalizes verified expertise; it does not invent it. Verification pass run 2026-06-14 (foreground WebSearch). Each principle now carries retrievable sources, a tier, confidence, verificationStatus, and any documented counter-practice. The event board's 'Weiss' persona maps to celebrity planner Mindy Weiss, whose PUBLISHED guidance is cited (NGW did not interview her — her published practice is the source). Ina Garten (Barefoot Contessa) added as a verified culinary-authority source. Items that would not source are honestly DOWN-TIERED (see k_help).

sources.name: Mindy Weiss (celebrity event planner — board 'Weiss')
sources.note: Published: greet guests with a drink; set the table the night before; don't run out to buy new decor — mix-and-match what you own; a winning dinner party is 'short, fun, heartfelt'.

sources.name: Ina Garten / Barefoot Contessa
sources.note: Published: 'Can I make this entire dinner ahead? If not, substitute dishes I can.' The Four-Dish Rule (one stovetop, one oven, one room-temp, one make-ahead). Never serve a recipe made for the first time. A written game plan.

sources.name: WebstaurantStore — catering portion guides
sources.note: 6–8 oz cooked protein/person for plated dinner (8–10 oz buffet/formal); order 10–15% extra for unexpected guests/hearty appetites.

sources.name: Wine-planning guides (LoveToKnow, Rosanna ETC)
sources.note: Half a bottle (~3 glasses) per person for a sit-down meal; err toward too much.

sources.name: Ice calculators (icecalculator.org, Reddy Ice)
sources.note: 1 lb (short indoor) / 1.5 lb (average) / 2+ lb (outdoor, long, cooler-heavy); +30–40% above 90°F.

sources.name: Food-allergy hosting etiquette (Ancient Harvest, Do Say Give)
sources.note: Ask for allergies/restrictions on the invitation or when guests RSVP; build the menu around them rather than retrofitting; a one-week follow-up flushes out the disclosures the first ask missed.

sources.name: Seating-plan guides (The Gourmet Host, The Kitchn)
sources.note: Above ~8 guests self-seating causes awkward shuffling; place cards recommended for tables of 6+.

sources.name: Food52 — prep a dinner party in advance
sources.note: Mise en place day-before; choose a starter prepped entirely ahead, one last-minute main, room-temp/hold sides, make-ahead dessert; make cocktails ahead.

sources.name: NGW planner synthesis
sources.note: NGW's own extrapolation; labeled, lower-confidence, must not be presented as authority.

principles.statement: Collect allergies and dietary restrictions on the invite / at RSVP, BEFORE the menu locks; build the menu around them; one-week follow-up for blanks. Always plate one safe vegetarian main.
principles.assumption: A guest may have an undisclosed restriction.
principles.counterPractice: Shared duty — guests should proactively disclose; some etiquette sources put the onus on the guest, not only the host.

principles.statement: Pick a menu you can largely make ahead; the host should plate, not cook, once guests arrive. Garten's Four-Dish Rule: one stovetop, one oven, one room-temp, one make-ahead. Never serve an untested recipe.
principles.assumption: A single host is both cook and host.

principles.statement: Don't cook a recipe for the first time for guests; serve tried-and-true dishes, or test the new one beforehand.
principles.assumption: Untested recipes hide time-sinks and failure points.

principles.statement: Plan ~6–8 oz cooked protein per guest for a plated main; +10–15% buffer for appetites/surprise guests.
principles.assumption: Plated main with sides; mixed appetites.

principles.statement: Plan ½ bottle (~3 glasses) of wine per drinking guest for a sit-down dinner; round up.
principles.assumption: Drinking guests, multi-hour seated dinner, wine primary.
principles.counterPractice: Casual guidance varies: ~2 glasses/guest for low-key dinners, or 1–2 glasses/guest/hour by duration.

principles.statement: Plan ~1.5 lb of ice per guest (1 lb short indoor, 2+ lb outdoor/long); add ~30–40% in heat above 90°F.
principles.counterPractice: Scales materially with weather, full-bar use, and cooler chilling — not a single fixed number.

principles.statement: Keep buffers: +10–15% food over headcount (cited), plus +1–2 place settings and +20 min cook-schedule slack and one appliance-independent rescue course (NGW practice).
principles.assumption: Real events vary from plan; +1s and breakage happen.
principles.verificationNote: Food buffer is cited; the +settings/+time/+rescue-course buffers remain NGW synthesis.

principles.statement: Use place cards / assigned seating for ~8+ guests; seat complementary energies together and split known conflicts.
principles.assumption: Mixed-familiarity guest list; host wants to shape the room.
principles.counterPractice: A documented minority prefers open seating for casual dinners; assigned seating can read as formal (Food52 host debate).

principles.statement: Greet every guest with a drink in hand; make cocktails ahead so you can welcome people instead of mixing. Set the table the night before so you look effortless.
principles.assumption: First impression + host presence shape the evening.

principles.statement: Surface the single highest-leverage concern with reassurance; redirect from low-stakes busywork (running out to buy new decor) toward the real lever. A thoughtful, present host beats a perfect one.
principles.assumption: First-time hosts face decision fatigue + perfectionism.
principles.verificationNote: Grounded in Weiss ('first mistake is running out to buy decorations'; 'short, fun, heartfelt') + Garten's written game-plan discipline; the specific prioritization logic is NGW's.

principles.statement: For 8+ guests, a post-party cleaner or bartender can be a high-ROI spend that buys the host's presence.
principles.assumption: Host values presence over cost and the budget allows.
principles.verificationNote: DOWN-TIERED after verification: did not source as consensus. The cited consensus (Garten/Food52/Weiss) achieves host presence via MAKE-AHEAD PREP, not hired help — so this is an NGW opinion, retained but flagged, not authority.

counterPracticeFound.position: Open/casual seating preferred by a documented minority of hosts; assigned seating can feel formal.
Casual events: ~2 glasses/guest or 1–2/hour rather than the ½-bottle seated-dinner figure.
Several etiquette sources frame disclosure as a shared host+guest responsibility, not the host's alone.
The cited consensus achieves host presence through make-ahead prep, not paid help — undercutting NGW's 'hire help' recommendation.

openAssumptions: Quantities assume a Western seated dinner; cuisine/culture shifts portions and beverage norms.
Cost ranges are USD and regionally generic — a market overlay is needed for accuracy.
Ice/wine heuristics assume drinking adults; adjust for dry events, kids, pregnancy-heavy lists.
verificationNext: Tier-1 professional-body sourcing (certified-planner curricula, a regional food-safety authority for allergen handling) would lift k_dietary/k_protein from trade/planner sources to a regulatory tier. Re-review on the engine's monthly cadence as norms shift.
